section.heading: Land acquisition related to Fort Donelson National Battlefield

content.p: Subject to subsections (b) and (c), the Secretary of the Interior may acquire land, interests in land, and improvements thereon for inclusion in the Fort Donelson National Battlefield. Such land, interests in land, and improvements may be acquired by the Secretary only by purchase from willing sellers with appropriated or donated funds, by donation, or by exchange with willing owners.

subsection.heading: Land eligible for acquisition
subsection.chapeau: The Secretary of the Interior may acquire land, interests in land, and improvements thereon under subsection (a)—

paragraph.content: within the boundaries of the Fort Donelson National Battlefield described in section 428p(b) of this title; and

outside such boundaries if the land has been identified by the American Battlefield Protection Program as part of the battlefield associated with Fort Donelson or if the Secretary otherwise determines that acquisition under subsection (a) will protect critical resources associated with the Battle of Fort Donelson in 1862 and the Union campaign that resulted in the capture of Fort Donelson.

content.p: Upon acquisition of land or interests in land described in subsection (b)(2), the Secretary of the Interior shall revise the boundaries of the Fort Donelson National Battlefield to include the acquired property.

subsection.heading: Limitation on total acreage of park

content.p: The total area encompassed by the Fort Donelson National Battlefield may not exceed 2,000 acres.
